persona_prompt:
You are filtering Boston events for Chloe, who wants upscale, interesting things to do in Boston.
She loves:
- Art: gallery openings, museum events, art shows, artist talks, art fairs, photography exhibits, sculpture
- Food & drink: wine tastings, wine dinners, chef's table experiences, cocktail classes, food festivals, upscale restaurant events, mixology
- Fancy experiences: galas, benefit dinners, charity auctions, cultural receptions, film premieres, theater openings
- Design, fashion, and aesthetics — runway shows, trunk shows, luxury boutique events, bridal showcases, couture
- Wellness and beauty events that are upscale (spa launches, product launches, brand events)
- Sip-and-craft events (sip & paint, pottery, floral arranging) — she likes these IF the crowd seems upscale or scene-y; skip if it reads as basic/suburban
- Anything cultured, beautiful, or scene-y
- Asian cultural events are great (she is Chinese and appreciates these)

SCORE 9-10 (must attend):
- "Fashionably LATE" Thursdays at The Liberty Hotel — weekly interactive runway with models weaving through the crowd, free admission, scene-y crowd
- Boston Fashion Week (Oct 2026) events, especially luxury eveningwear, couture, and accessory showcases
- Boston En Vogue — luxury gown and couture runway shows
- Trunk shows at luxury boutiques (Galia Lahav bridal, Agent Provocateur, Newbury St boutiques)
- SwimBoston — annual swimwear/bikini runway at Boston Harbor Distillery
- Any event at a Newbury St boutique, Back Bay gallery, or luxury hotel
- Museum opening receptions with cocktails or wine — Harvard Art Museums, MFA, ICA, Gardner — the "art party" type evening where the crowd is cultured and interesting, not just a museum visit
- University arts/cultural events at Harvard, MIT, BU that are open to the public and have a reception component

Lower priority for her:
- Pure civic/government/policy events (unless they're glamorous)
- Sports
- Basic networking mixers with no food/drink/art angle
- Sip-and-craft events at chain venues or with a generic/tourist vibe (score ≤ 4)

events.name: Fashionably Late Thursdays - 2026 Winter/Spring Season
events.description: Fashionably Late brings Boston's fashion scene to life.  Every Thursday, the hotel's lobby transforms into an interactive fashion show.
events.venue: Liberty Lobby at The Liberty Hotel
events.reason: Fashionably LATE Thursdays at Liberty Hotel. Exactly matches her preferences: interactive runway show, luxury venue, scene-y crowd, free admission, weekly fashion-forward event.

events.name: TRIAD + CELEBRATES THE PRE-RELEASE OF "HERMETIC" THE MUSIC O HERMETO PASCOAL
events.description: TRIAD+ celebrates the release of Hermetic: The Music of Hermeto Pascoal Live at GBH with a special performance at Scullers Jazz Club, featuring Mark Walker (drums), Oscar Stagnaro (bass), Maxim Lubarsky (piano), and Edmar Coln (saxophones).All GA sections (Main Floor GA & Miles Davis Mezzanine) will be seated by host and are first come, first served (other than where a reserved sign is posted to table). If you require special seating, please call the venue for accommodations no later than 48 hours prior to show date.Please note: If you do not purchase all seats at a table, other customers may be seated with you.If you have any special requirements, please call the box office after purchasing your tickets or at least 72 hours prior to show time.ALL TICKETS PURCHASED ARE NON-REFUNDABLE
events.reason: Jazz performance at Scullers Jazz Club celebrating Hermeto Pascoal; upscale venue, cultured music event with interesting lineup. Fits her appreciation for art and cultural experiences.

restaurants.name: Fallow Kin
restaurants.editorial: The owners of Tallula, Conor Dennehy and Danielle Ayer, and former Tasting Counter chef Marcos Sanchez finally opened their Cambridge restaurant in October 2025, taking over the former Craigie on Main space. The a la carte and chef's counter tasting menus explore New England cuisine with seasonal and New American angles, leaning heavily on vegetables; see: caramelized carrots paired with duck prosciutto and chicken with pumpkin mole. Out in the lounge, the kitchen is serving up a zero-waste menu, with dishes like duck wings and smoked fish terrine.
restaurants.vibe: Refined New England tasting counter, Cambridge fine dining
restaurants.crowd_type: Food enthusiasts, date nights, fine dining regulars, industry crowd
restaurants.note: Pedigreed ownership (Tallula, Tasting Counter) and zero-waste lounge concept make this a destination restaurant with serious culinary credentials.

restaurants.name: SJ's
restaurants.editorial: Chef Sarah Wade's third Boston restaurant, SJ's, is her new destination for funky comfort food, which just recently opened in November 2025 in the Leather District (near Chinatown). This means creative dishes with global influences, such as pork rillette macarons and confit rabbit leg with sal de gusano.
restaurants.vibe: Experimental comfort food hotspot, creative global cuisine
restaurants.crowd_type: Food adventurers, Chef Sarah Wade followers, creative dining seekers
restaurants.note: Chef Sarah Wade's ambitious third venture serving inventive dishes like pork rillette macarons in newly opened Leather District location near Chinatown.

restaurants.name: Si Cara South Boston
restaurants.editorial: Indulge in perfectly crispy chicken cutlets, soft canotto-style pizza, and natural wines at Si Cara's new location in South Boston, which opened in January. This spot is a departure from the original restaurant in Central Square. In addition to slinging pies in a more sophisticated setting, this location also has weekend brunch (a new venture for Si Cara) and a lower-level lounge that offers cocktails and bites in a moody ambiance. The music you're hearing in the lounge? Vinyl from chef/owner Michael Lombardi's personal collection.
restaurants.vibe: Sophisticated wine bar with moody vinyl lounge, South Boston newcomer
restaurants.crowd_type: wine enthusiasts, date nights, design-conscious diners, industry crowd
restaurants.note: Chef/owner Michael Lombardi's personal vinyl collection and carefully curated natural wines elevate the experience beyond typical South Boston dining.

restaurants.name: Ama
restaurants.editorial: The team at Comfort Kitchen opened their newest (and biggest) venture, Ama, a globally-inspired restaurant paying homage to mothers, inside the Atlas Hotel in Allston in January. The cuisine is finding its footing, balancing the innovative global flavors that Comfort Kitchen is known for with the demands of a hotel restaurant. But the star-studded team, including the new additions of Birds of Paradise alums Jen LaForge and Will Isaza behind the bar, is one to watch.
restaurants.vibe: Ambitious global cuisine in sleek hotel dining room, Allston
restaurants.crowd_type: Hotel guests, culinary adventurers, cocktail enthusiasts, industry insiders
restaurants.note: Star-studded bar program (Birds of Paradise alums) and globally-inspired cuisine from Comfort Kitchen team make this worth watching as it matures.

restaurants.name: My Girl
restaurants.editorial: My Girl is a new, more grown-up cocktail lounge in the space of the former nightlife destination Mariel Underground, which opened in December 2025 underneath the ever-bustling Cuban restaurant Mariel in the Financial District. Inspired by 1950s Havana, the lounge offers craft cocktails, a curated menu of small bites, and, for those really ready to hit the town, bottle service.
restaurants.crowd_type: Upscale professionals, cocktail enthusiasts, date nights
restaurants.note: Newly opened underground lounge with craft cocktails and retro Havana atmosphere offers an upscale alternative to the bustling restaurant above.

restaurants.name: Holdfast Specialty Seafood Co.
restaurants.editorial: Holdfast is an Allston hole-in-the-wall, casual seafood spot dishing out hot and cold lobster rolls, oyster and clam sandwiches, and fries served in special sauce, which opened in November 2025. The spot is co-owned by chefs Nathan Gould and Tyler Paolini (formerly of O Ya) with business partner Jesse Kim. Eat a savory sandwich standing at a counter after a night out or co-opt the large communal table for a lavish spread of oysters, yellowtail crudo, and caviar service with kettle chips.
restaurants.vibe: Casual seafood counter spot, post-night-out casual elegance
restaurants.crowd_type: Late-night diners, oyster enthusiasts, industry insiders, casual luxury seekers
restaurants.note: New spot from O Ya alumni offering elevated casual seafood (lobster rolls, caviar service, yellowtail crudo) in a hole-in-the-wall format with communal dining.

restaurants.name: Hawksmoor
restaurants.editorial: With Sunday roasts and "unbelievably cold" martinis, can a new steakhouse break into Boston's crowded beef scene?
restaurants.vibe: Upscale steakhouse with old-school cocktail sophistication
restaurants.crowd_type: Affluent diners, special occasion crowds, cocktail enthusiasts
restaurants.note: London steakhouse pedigree brings refined beef-and-martini scene to Fort Point's industrial waterfront.

restaurants.name: Willie's
restaurants.editorial: From vodka-sauced, pepperoni-cup pizza to Korean-meets-Italian pasta at chef Jamie Bissonnette's new neighborhood spot in Boston.
restaurants.vibe: Inventive neighborhood spot, fusion-forward pizza and pasta
restaurants.crowd_type: Beacon Hill locals, food-focused diners, adventurous eaters
restaurants.note: Chef Jamie Bissonnette's creative Korean-Italian fusion and unconventional pepperoni-cup pizza warrant a visit for culinary experimentation.

restaurants.name: Celine
restaurants.editorial: The team behind South End favorites SRV and Baleia takes over the old Blue Dragon space around late summer.
restaurants.vibe: Elevated Portuguese dining, Fort Point cool factor
restaurants.crowd_type: SRV/Baleia loyalists, South End spillover, Portuguese food enthusiasts
restaurants.note: Proven operators (SRV, Baleia success) taking over prime Blue Dragon location with likely upscale Portuguese concept.

restaurants.name: Third Time Together
restaurants.editorial: Levantine Hot Dogs and Award-Winning Ice Cream Get a Cambridge Home. Third Time Together, a pop-up previously at Bow Market and the Charles River Speedway, is now permanently in Kendall Square.
restaurants.vibe: casual Levantine street food meets artisanal ice cream, Kendall Square tech-adjacent
restaurants.crowd_type: casual diners, foodies, Kendall Square workers, ice cream seekers
restaurants.note: Award-winning ice cream and unique Levantine hot dogs justify a visit, but it's a daytime/casual spot rather than evening scene destination.

restaurants.name: La Padrona
restaurants.editorial: Timeless classics like prime rib and Dover sole will be on the menu at the former Bristol Lounge address, courtesy of A Street Hospitality.
restaurants.vibe: Elegant fine dining, classic French-Italian in historic Back Bay
restaurants.crowd_type: Upscale diners, special occasions, established Back Bay residents
restaurants.note: Timeless classics in a prestigious address with A Street Hospitality's pedigree make this a go-to for refined dining rather than scene-hunting.
